forecasts.nextMove: Hold for rest of 2026 — wait and assess
Will 'do what it considers necessary' — door open for more hikes

rba.note: 3rd hike of 2026 = 4.35% (was 4.10%). Middle East oil crisis fuelling inflation. Westpac sees 4.85% by August.

employment.note: March stable at 4.3%. April data releases 21/05. Labour market resilient despite rate hikes.

inflation.note: Headline jumped to 4.6% (from 3.7%). Housing +6.5%, Transport +8.9%, Fuel +24.2%. Trimmed mean 3.3% still well above target.

wages.note: Wages 3.4% vs inflation 4.6% = real wages going BACKWARDS. Cost of living squeeze.

migration.forecast2026: Treasury: NOM to 260,000 in 2025-26, settling near 225,000 by 2026-27
migration.note: Demand pressure continues. WA/QLD/SA still leading. Net migration sustains rental and housing demand.

approvals.vsTarget: 938k forecast vs 1.2M Housing Accord target = 22% shortfall over 5 years
approvals.note: March approvals DOWN 10.5%. Council projects 938k vs 1.2M target. Persistent undersupply POSITIVE for existing values.

vacancy.note: National up to 1.2% (from 1.0%) but still very tight. Perth/Adelaide/Brisbane critically low — rents continuing to rise.

medians.note: Two-speed market widening: 24pt gap Perth (+26%) vs Melbourne (+2%). Perth/Brisbane/Adelaide at record highs. Sydney still below Nov 2025 peak. Q1: Perth +7.3%.

clearanceRates.note: Sydney/Melbourne 58-60% (below long-term avg). Adelaide 75% strongest. Lower clearance = more buyer leverage.

lending.apraChanges: DTI cap: max 20% of new loans at 6x+ income, active since Feb 2026. 3% serviceability buffer retained.
lending.note: Investor rates 5.84-6.85% after May hike. Assessment rate ~9.5%. DTI cap binding on investors (10% already above 6x).

negativeGearing.change: REMOVED for established residential properties acquired after 7:30pm AEST 12 May 2026
exemptions: Properties owned before 12 May 2026 (grandfathered for life)
Properties under contract before 12 May 2026 (awaiting settlement)
negativeGearing.impact: Investors buying established houses after 12/05/2026 CANNOT offset rental losses against other income. New builds remain fully negative-gearable.

capitalGainsTax.change: 50% CGT discount REMOVED, replaced with cost base indexation + 30% minimum tax on net gains
exemptions: Gains realised before 1 July 2027 keep 50% discount
New residential property buyers can CHOOSE between old (50% discount) or new regime on disposal
capitalGainsTax.impact: Established property capital gains taxed at min 30% on indexed gains. New builds get choice = better outcome.

stampDuty.QLD: ~$25k on $550k investor (+ land tax for 2nd QLD property)
stampDuty.note: Rates unchanged in 2026-27 budget but rising property prices = higher dollar cost

risks.risk: Negative gearing REMOVED for established homes acquired after 12/05/2026
risks.impact: CRITICAL: Established houses bought now CANNOT be negatively geared. Must be cash flow positive day 1 OR pivot to new builds.
Established property gains taxed at min 30% indexed. New builds get choice of regime.
Each 25bp cuts borrowing capacity ~3%. Investor rates pushing 7%.
More hikes likely. No pivot until inflation back to target.
Cost of living + further inflation pressure.
Window closing — sub-$800k WA/QLD suburbs being priced out fast.

risks.risk: Investor flight to new builds post-budget
risks.impact: Established demand softens. New build prices may rise.

summary: MAJOR REGIME CHANGE 12 May 2026: Federal Budget killed negative gearing on new established-property purchases and removed CGT 50% discount from July 2027. Macro tightening: RBA 4.35% (3rd hike), inflation 4.6%, investor rates 5.84-6.85%. Despite headwinds, Perth +26% and Brisbane +19.7% continue on supply shortage. STRATEGY SHIFT: Either (1) buy new builds to keep negative gearing + CGT choice, or (2) buy established that's cash-flow-positive from day one. The 'negative gear + wait for growth' play is DEAD for established houses bought today.
warnings: 🚨 NEGATIVE GEARING REMOVED for established homes after 12 May 2026 (2 days ago). Existing portfolio grandfathered.
🚨 CGT 50% discount GONE from 1 July 2027 — replaced with 30% min tax on indexed gains. New builds get choice.
🚨 RBA at 4.35% — Westpac forecasts 4.85% by August. Investor rates pushing 7%. Confirm serviceability with broker BEFORE offers.
⚠ Perth +26% annual = sub-$800k suburbs disappearing. Act decisively or move to next-tier.
⚠ APRA DTI 6x income cap binding on investors. Get pre-approval before house hunting.
✅ POSITIVE: Housing undersupply persists (938k vs 1.2M target). Established prices supported.
💡 STRATEGY: New builds in growth corridors now structurally favoured. Reconsider H&L packages vs established.
